summary: Partial coverage: connector outage, Temporal resume, provider schema-fail, KnowledgeWriter conflict, GBrain divergence, and DB-unavailable/migration are well specified (PRD LIFE-1..6, OBS-1/2, §14.5, §16.1; DOMAIN_MODEL state machines). But four production failure paths are absent from BOTH draft and source docs — Keychain locked, supervised-worker crash/restart policy, Temporal process unavailable, and partial cross-store deletion compensation — plus three under-specified runtime degradations (provider fallback, GBrain-process-unavailable retrieval, outbox give-up/dead-letter).

findings.finding: Keychain locked / unavailable is an undefined failure path. Secrets live only in macOS Keychain (DECISIONS.md ADR; REQ-S-003), so a locked Keychain (screen-locked, fresh boot before unlock, or denied access prompt) makes every cloud provider key and every connector OAuth token unreachable at once — silently failing all provider jobs and connector syncs. No degraded behavior, retry-on-unlock, or user prompt is specified.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §5/§13 and DATA_MODEL.md:18 reference Keychain secrets but never a locked/unavailable path; grep of PRD (lines 65, 380, 959, 1083) shows Keychain defined as the store but no locked-state error path; THREAT_MODEL.md trust-boundary table has no Keychain-unavailable row.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a SecretsPort failure path: on Keychain locked/denied, mark affected providers/connectors degraded in System Health (distinct OBS-2-class item), hold dependent jobs in a retryable state, and re-attempt on unlock (wire to LIFE-6 wake/power hooks). Define whether the app prompts for unlock or waits passively (owner UX call).

findings.finding: Supervised control-plane worker crash has no restart/supervision policy. The draft says Electron main 'supervises' the Node/TS worker but never defines the restart strategy, backoff, crash-loop detection/give-up, what the renderer shows while the worker is down, or the disposition of any side effect in flight at crash time. LIFE-1 covers the single-instance lease and stale-lock recovery but not worker respawn semantics.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §1, §13 ('supervises control-plane worker') and DECISIONS.md row 'Process model'; CONSTRAINTS.md/DATA_MODEL grep shows no 'supervis'/'restart'/'crash' entries; PRD grep for 'worker.*(crash|restart|supervis)' returns only resume-on-restart (lines 698, 1003), not a supervision policy.
findings.proposed_fix: Specify the main-process supervision contract: restart-with-bounded-backoff, crash-loop threshold that surfaces a System Health/UI 'worker down' state instead of looping, lease re-acquisition on respawn (LIFE-1), and that in-flight side effects are recovered via Temporal resume + §16.1 envelope on the new worker.

findings.finding: Temporal local server unavailable has no degraded mode. §4 defines DB-unavailable (degraded mode, queue, System Health) and migration-mismatch (block + repair) paths, but the parallel dependency — the local Temporal dev server failing to start or crashing — has no equivalent. Since all product workflows and durable schedules run on Temporal, its unavailability halts the entire control plane with no defined surfacing or recovery.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4 'Failure modes' covers DB only; §13 'Worker starts/connects to local Temporal dev server' with no failure branch; §9 lists no Temporal-down path. PRD grep for 'temporal.*(down|unavail|crash)' finds none.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a Temporal-unavailable failure mode mirroring §4 DB handling: worker enters degraded mode, blocks workflow dispatch, surfaces a distinct System Health item, retries connection with backoff, and reports a repair path if the embedded server cannot start.

findings.finding: Partial cross-store deletion has no compensation/saga design. REQ-F-013 and PRD §20 require deletion across Markdown + GBrain index/graph + event store + read models + external links to be 'atomically (or compensatably)' purged with no orphans/resurrection, and Flow 7 lists failure states (partial purge, GBrain purge failure, dangling external refs) — but the draft only names 'Retention/deletion' as a §9 workflow with zero detail on ordering, compensation, idempotent re-drive, or crash recovery mid-deletion. There is no deletion state machine in DOMAIN_MODEL either.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §9 lists 'Retention/deletion' with no design; REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-F-013; USER_FLOWS.md Flow 7 'Failure States: partial purge, write conflict, GBrain purge failure, dangling external refs'; PRD lines 1074, 1114 (acceptance) demand compensatable purge; DOMAIN_MODEL.md state machines (lines 40,51,59,63) have no deletion lifecycle.
findings.proposed_fix: Specify a deletion saga: deterministic deletion plan, ordered steps (Markdown tombstone via KnowledgeWriter as commit point → GBrain purge/re-index → event-store tombstone → read-model + external-ref reconciliation), per-step idempotency, compensating/retry states for partial failure surfaced in System Health, and a re-index-does-not-resurrect invariant.

findings.finding: Provider-unavailable runtime fallback is undefined. The provider matrix lists allowedProviders per workspace but no ordering or fallback chain, and Flow 8 lists 'provider unavailable' as a failure state without a runtime disposition. When the critical-path provider (Claude) is down mid-job, it is unspecified whether the broker fails the job (failed_retryable/terminal), retries, or falls back to another allowed provider — and provider fallback could change egress class, which interacts with Employer-Work egress policy.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §7 'Select runtime/provider/model' and Appendix A ProviderMatrix (allowedProviders, no order); USER_FLOWS.md Flow 8 'Failure States: provider unavailable'; DOMAIN_MODEL.md:63 has provider_selected/failed_retryable but no fallback rule; OQ-003/OQ-004 cover model/budget defaults, not failover.
findings.proposed_fix: Define the broker's provider-unavailable behavior: whether an ordered fallback within the egress-compatible allowed set is attempted (and that fallback must re-check egress class), the retry/backoff bound, and the terminal disposition (job → failed_terminal + System Health) when no provider is available.

findings.finding: GBrain process unavailable at retrieval time has no defined degradation. The draft addresses GBrain divergence/parity and the write-through containment fallback (read-only) thoroughly, but not the case where the GBrain process/sidecar is simply down when a workflow needs retrieval/graph context. Flow 1 preconditions say 'GBrain workspace brain available or degraded' yet 'degraded' retrieval semantics (block the closeout, proceed with reduced context, or queue) are never specified.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §6 covers parity/divergence and read-only fallback only; USER_FLOWS.md Flow 1 preconditions 'available or degraded' with no semantics; DOMAIN_MODEL.md:63 AgentJob lifecycle has no GBrain-unavailable branch; PRD §17 OBS-1 surfaces 'GBrain health' but not workflow disposition.
findings.proposed_fix: Specify GBrain-unavailable behavior per retrieval-dependent workflow: retry with backoff, surface GBrain-down in System Health, and define whether the meeting-closeout/brief blocks (queues) or proceeds in a clearly-flagged reduced-context mode that cannot silently degrade evidence requirements.

findings.finding: Outbox retry give-up / dead-letter policy is undefined. DATA_MODEL enumerates four outboxes and DOMAIN_MODEL defines failed_terminal/cancelled_budget terminal states, but no doc defines the transition criteria — max attempts, backoff ceiling, or poison-item handling — that move a retryable item to terminal. PRD OBS-2 keeps failure items visible 'until resolved or acknowledged' with no dead-letter path, so a permanently-failing write (e.g., precondition that never clears) or a never-validating provider output could retry indefinitely.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md:30 (four outboxes), DOMAIN_MODEL.md:40/51/63 (failed_terminal, cancelled_budget) but no give-up criteria; CONSTRAINTS.md:64 flags 'provider-specific timeout/retry defaults' as an OPEN question; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4/§7/§8 mention 'retry' without bounds; PRD OBS-2 (line 558) has no dead-letter.
findings.proposed_fix: Define per-outbox retry policy: max attempts / max staleness, backoff ceiling, and a dead-letter/terminal transition that surfaces a distinct System Health item and stops looping (mapping to DOMAIN_MODEL failed_terminal), so no item retries forever.

findings.finding: The draft refers to 'outboxes' generically (§1, §4) without enumerating them, even though DATA_MODEL already lists four distinct outboxes with different retry semantics. The final ARCHITECTURE.md should name them and the PRD's two-channel distinction (failed Markdown write-through vs GBrain sync lag, both on the §16.1 retry outbox).
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4 'outboxes' (unenumerated) vs DATA_MODEL.md:30 'KnowledgeWriter retries, Tool Gateway retries, connector retries, GBrain sync retries'; PRD line 874 distinguishes failed write-through from GBrain sync lag sharing the §16.1 outbox.
findings.proposed_fix: In §4, enumerate the outbox taxonomy (KnowledgeWriter write outbox, Tool Gateway write outbox, connector read/sync queue, GBrain re-index outbox), each with its retry/backoff/dead-letter policy and its distinct OBS-2 System Health surface.

findings.finding: Failure-mode handling is scattered (only §4 has an explicit 'Failure modes' block) while DOMAIN_MODEL already defines the relevant terminal/error states (provider_failed, schema_rejected, write_conflict, cancelled_budget, failed_retryable, failed_terminal). The binding ARCHITECTURE.md would benefit from one consolidated Failure Modes & Error Paths section that maps each named failure (connector outage, provider unavailable/schema-fail/budget, worker/Temporal crash, GBrain unavailable/divergent, KnowledgeWriter conflict, DB unavailable/migration, partial deletion, Keychain locked) to its detection, retry/outbox/compensation, terminal state, and System Health item.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md has explicit 'Failure modes' only in §4; DOMAIN_MODEL.md:51/63 already names the error states; PRD OBS-2 (line 558) enumerates required distinct failure classes.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a consolidated 'Failure Modes & Error Paths' section to ARCHITECTURE.md cross-referencing DOMAIN_MODEL state machines and OBS-2 classes, with a table: failure → detection → retry/outbox/compensation → terminal disposition → System Health surface.
